hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/ECL3, while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 electrostatic complementarity, thereby increasing selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: strong SSTR2 binding can be achieved, but selectivity over SSTR3/SSTR5 remains a challenge. The next iteration should focus on disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14).

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are known to contribute to off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing design on these positions can help disrupt conserved-pocket contacts while preserving key pharmacophore residues.
Introducing charged or polar residues at these positions can disrupt hydrophobic or conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5, reducing off-target binding while preserving SSTR2 selectivity.
